metadata.subject: Arts Education
metadata.description: Media Arts 10 explores the creation, analysis, and critical understanding of media — including photography, video, digital imagery, and interactive media. Students develop technical skills in digital tools and platforms, critically examine how media constructs meaning and shapes culture, and create their own media works with intentional artistic and communicative purpose. First Peoples media artists and storytelling traditions are central to the course. This course opens pathways to Media Arts 11 and 12.
metadata.general_description: An interdisciplinary senior arts course developing technical media production skills, critical media literacy, and original artistic voice through the creation and analysis of photography, video, digital art, and interactive media.

big_ideas.statement: Media arts offer unique ways of communicating ideas, perspectives, and cultural values.
big_ideas.elaboration: Media — from photography and film to social media and interactive installations — shapes how we understand reality, construct identity, and communicate across cultures. Learning to create and critically read media is essential in the contemporary world.

big_ideas.statement: Creative growth requires patience, readiness to take risks, and willingness to try new approaches.
big_ideas.elaboration: Digital and media arts are iterative — requiring experimentation, revision, and the courage to try unfamiliar tools and approaches. Learning new technologies and creative processes takes patience and persistence.

big_ideas.statement: Media arts are shaped by and reflect the cultures and contexts in which they are created.
big_ideas.elaboration: Every media work is made from a cultural perspective — reflecting the values, biases, and choices of its creator. Critically analyzing media requires asking who made it, for whom, and why.

big_ideas.statement: Artists challenge established perspectives and expose us to new viewpoints through media.
big_ideas.elaboration: Media artists have the power to amplify underrepresented voices, challenge dominant narratives, and create new imaginative possibilities — making media arts a powerful vehicle for social commentary and cultural change.

competencies.raw: Develop technical skills in media arts tools and processes; apply media arts elements and principles to create original works; explore relationships between identity, culture, and media; take creative risks and experiment with new technologies.
i_can_statements: I can develop technical skills in digital photography, video editing, and image manipulation.
I can apply media arts elements — composition, lighting, framing, sound, and editing — to create original works.
I can explore how identity, culture, and community appear in my media work.
I can take creative risks and experiment with new technologies and media platforms.

competencies.raw: Analyze and respond to media works using media-specific vocabulary; apply feedback to improve media projects; examine media in social, cultural, and historical context; critically evaluate how media constructs meaning.
i_can_statements: I can analyze media works using accurate vocabulary: composition, framing, editing, sound design, and narrative.
I can apply feedback to revise and strengthen my media projects.
I can critically examine how media constructs meaning, represents identities, and reflects cultural values.
I can examine media works in their social, cultural, and historical contexts.

competencies.raw: Publish and share media works for appropriate audiences; use media language to convey specific meanings; document creative process through project logs and reflection.
i_can_statements: I can publish and share media works for specific audiences using appropriate platforms.
I can use visual, auditory, and textual elements of media language to convey specific meanings.
I can document my creative process through project logs, storyboards, and reflective writing.

competencies.raw: Demonstrate ethical responsibility in media creation and sharing; explore First Peoples media arts; examine how media shapes culture and society; build community connections through media storytelling.
i_can_statements: I can demonstrate ethical responsibility in media creation — understanding copyright, consent, and digital citizenship.
I can engage with First Peoples media artists and storytelling traditions respectfully.
I can use media arts to give voice to social, environmental, and community issues.
I can examine how media shapes cultural values, public opinion, and social identity.

areas.name: Photography
areas.raw: Photography: composition, exposure, and digital manipulation.
topics: Camera basics: aperture, shutter speed, ISO, and exposure triangle
Compositional principles: rule of thirds, leading lines, depth of field, and framing
Lighting techniques: natural light, artificial light, and understanding shadows
Photo editing: basics of Lightroom, Photoshop, or equivalent — cropping, colour correction, and retouching
Documentary and artistic photography: genre exploration

areas.name: Video Production
areas.raw: Video production: shot types, editing, sound, and narrative.
topics: Camera techniques: shot types (wide, medium, close-up), angles, and camera movement
Pre-production: concept development, storyboarding, and scriptwriting
Production: filming, directing, and basic crew roles
Post-production: editing software basics, cuts, transitions, and pacing
Sound design: dialogue, music, and sound effects in video
Short documentary and narrative film forms

areas.name: Digital Art and Design
areas.raw: Digital art, graphic design, and interactive media.
topics: Digital image creation: illustration and design using digital tools
Typography: font selection, hierarchy, and design with text
Graphic design principles: layout, grid, and visual communication
Introduction to interactive and generative media
Social media as a creative and critical platform

areas.name: Critical Media Literacy
areas.raw: How media constructs meaning, represents culture, and shapes identity.
topics: Media construction: how all media are constructed with choices and purposes
Representation: who is shown, who is absent, and how groups are portrayed
First Peoples media artists and Indigenous storytelling in film and digital media
Media and identity: how media shapes self-concept and social norms
Digital ethics: consent, privacy, copyright, and responsible sharing
Media and social change: how artists use media for activism and community storytelling
